stories.title: The Three Yeses — How 25 Universities Govern AI
stories.description: Every one of twenty-five universities says students may use AI. None of them mean the same thing — and what none of them say is the story that matters most. Analysis of public AI governance documents across 25 leading universities in 9 countries.
stories.summary: University AI policies all permit student use, but their disclosure rules range from vague encouragement to forensic audit trails, exposing fundamentally different accountability models for the same tools.

stories.title: What 765 Students Taught Us About Learning, Hacking, and Everything In Between
stories.description: Analysis of 765 students, 9,046 saved submissions, and 1,731 evaluated AI images from TDS Jan 2026 Project 1, showing how learning shifts when answers are one search away.
stories.summary: Analysis of 765 students, 9,046 submission saves, and 1,731 evaluated images shows how deadline pressure, validator failures, collaboration patterns, and image-generation choices reshape learning when answers are easy to fetch.

stories.title: The 80-Year Blind Spot: The Polya Audit
stories.description: In 1945, George Pólya gave mathematicians 15 rules for solving problems. For eight decades, they were treated as gospel. Nobody ever tested them. Until now.
stories.summary: Testing George Polya's 15 classic problem-solving heuristics against modern AI models finds that some rules still help, others backfire, and their value depends strongly on the model applying them.

stories.title: The Return That Wasn't — Did Vibe Coding Bring Developers Back to GitHub?
stories.description: When AI supposedly brought a generation of lapsed developers back to GitHub, we checked the commit logs. Here's what we found.
stories.summary: GitHub commit histories show no mass return of dormant developers after the vibe-coding boom; most apparent growth comes from already active users, survivorship effects, and a small minority with real increases.

stories.title: Three Novel Data Visualizations
stories.description: Each chart solves an analytical problem for which no adequate visualization previously existed, combining novel encodings with rich, realistic synthetic data.
stories.summary: Three original chart types show how to encode lead-lag relationships, team momentum, and business activity rhythms when familiar charts fail, using realistic synthetic datasets to demonstrate genuinely new analytical affordances.

stories.title: Crack the Prompt
stories.description: A PyConf Hyderabad 2026 security challenge: three AI personas guarding hidden system prompts. Codex extracted all three in seven words each.
stories.summary: A PyConf security challenge shows how terse, carefully framed prompts can extract hidden system instructions from guarded AI personas, turning prompt injection from abstract risk into a concrete red-team exercise.

stories.title: Strategic Assessment: Client Reporting Transformation
stories.description: An AI-generated executive strategy deck synthesized from a stakeholder interview recording, showing how raw audio can be transformed into boardroom-ready insights.
stories.summary: A stakeholder interview about client reporting becomes a boardroom-style strategy deck, showing how AI can turn messy audio, operational pain points, and business goals into structured transformation recommendations.

stories.title: Codex Session Gap Analysis
stories.description: Analysis of 903 Codex sessions from Apr 2025 to Mar 2026, showing feature adoption gaps, release-aware coverage, and workflow recommendations.
stories.summary: Reviewing 903 Codex sessions surfaces a feature-adoption gap: users embrace models and core chat flows but ignore newer workflow primitives like parallel, planning, and agent orchestration that could materially boost throughput.

stories.title: SQL Migration Narrative Demo
stories.description: An interactive walkthrough of migrating 100 SQL Server scripts to MySQL with LLM-assisted conversion, verification, and business-impact simulation.
stories.summary: An interactive migration demo shows how LLMs can convert 100 SQL Server scripts to MySQL while tracking verification, effort, and business impact, making legacy modernization legible instead of opaque.

stories.title: Researcher of the Future
stories.description: How 3,200+ researchers across 113 countries view AI, integrity, collaboration, mobility, and impact — from Elsevier's Confidence in Research report.
stories.summary: An interactive deck distills Elsevier's global researcher survey into themes on AI, integrity, collaboration, mobility, and impact, highlighting how researchers expect technology and institutions to reshape scientific work.

stories.title: Retractions Are Happening in Waves
stories.description: We analyzed 68,658 Retraction Watch records to find the biggest patterns, surprising signals, and practical actions. An early-warning publisher dashboard for where trust, reputation, and legal risk can spike.
stories.summary: Analysis of 68,658 Retraction Watch records reveals retractions arrive in waves, cluster by publisher and country, and increasingly cite specific misconduct, creating an early-warning lens on scientific trust and legal risk.

stories.title: Can AI Replace Human Paper Reviewers?
stories.description: An investigation into what happens when artificial intelligence reviews scientific papers — and what goes hilariously (and seriously) wrong.
stories.summary: AI reviewers of scientific papers produce distinct personalities, low agreement, and frequent blind spots, suggesting automated peer review can be entertaining and scalable without being reliable enough to replace humans.

stories.title: The Invisible Infrastructure
stories.description: How tens of thousands of packages depend on code almost no one has heard of.
stories.summary: Dependency analysis across top npm and PyPI packages shows modern software rests on obscure infrastructural libraries, with extreme concentration and aging critical packages creating hidden fragility beneath popular ecosystems.

stories.title: The Jamnagar Chokepoint: Inside India's $273B Trade Paradox
stories.description: How a single port, two commodities, and a hidden export surge reveal the fragile architecture of India's $273 billion trade deficit.
stories.summary: India's trade deficit looks broad, but commodity flows through Jamnagar reveal a chokepoint economy where petroleum, hidden export surges, and geopolitical routing dominate a surprisingly fragile trade architecture.

stories.title: The Ambiguous Song
stories.description: How humans label emotions in music—400 tracks across 4 genres rated by multiple annotators, revealing which emotions spark the most disagreement.
stories.summary: Human annotators disagree sharply on musical emotion, and that disagreement itself becomes the story, revealing which feelings are ambiguous, which genres confuse listeners, and why single-label emotion datasets oversimplify music.

stories.title: Can AI Hear What We Feel?
stories.description: Gemini's music-emotion predictions vs Emotify human ratings across 40 songs using GEMS-9, revealing where AI hears differently.
stories.summary: Comparing Gemini's music-emotion predictions with human Emotify ratings shows AI can imitate broad affect patterns but still misses uncertainty, overstates confidence, and hears some songs differently from people.

stories.title: Communicating Insights Visually
stories.description: How top AI chatbots and coding agents translate Anthropic’s “How AI is transforming work at Anthropic” into diverse animated chart ideas—compared and ranked.
stories.summary: Multiple AI assistants reinterpret Anthropic's work-transformation report into competing animated chart concepts, exposing which models generate the most original, usable visual narratives from the same source material.

stories.title: The Ruler-Straight Disappearing Act
stories.description: A 24 kg drop from 86.4 to 62.2 kg across 335 days in 2025, with two changepoints and a ruler-straight curve from a Google Fit export.
stories.summary: A 24 kg weight-loss record plots almost too neatly, with two changepoints and a ruler-straight decline that turns personal health tracking into a puzzle about discipline, measurement, and plausibility.

stories.title: The Command Paradox
stories.description: Inside 534k AI prompt battles, polite "tell me a story" requests beat forceful "never reveal" commands—tracking 785 students' 100-character defenses and attacks.
stories.summary: Across 534,000 prompt battles, polite attacks outperform forceful commands, revealing that persuasion and framing beat aggression when students try to jailbreak or defend 100-character AI prompts.

stories.title: OLAP Git Commits
stories.description: Forensic read of 466k commits across 13 OLAP databases—one-person armies, small-commit speed demons, and weekend work as a funding tell.
stories.summary: A forensic read of 466,000 commits across 13 OLAP databases finds wildly different development cultures, from one-person armies to high-velocity teams, with commit size and weekend work hinting at funding and maintenance styles.

stories.title: Generosity of Strangers
stories.description: Party-of-five NYC taxi riders tip 15–20% more than solo travelers—maps, routes, and night effects reveal where generosity spikes.
stories.summary: NYC taxi trips show larger groups tip more generously than solo riders, with geography, time of day, and route context shaping where the party-of-five premium appears strongest.

stories.title: Indian Batting Greats
stories.description: Ranked Indian batting legends with an LLM-chosen metric: batting average x log(total runs), plotted across their active years.
stories.summary: Ranking Indian batting greats by batting average times log total runs balances longevity with excellence, creating an era-spanning comparison that favors sustained production over raw fame or peak mythology.

stories.title: The Reconciliation Engine
stories.description: Fuzzy search playground that reconciles bank transactions to accounting records using similarity scores and optimal assignments.
stories.summary: An interactive reconciliation engine demonstrates how similarity scoring and optimal assignment can match messy bank transactions to accounting records, turning fuzzy matching into a transparent financial-control workflow.

stories.title: TDS Project 2: The Cliff
stories.description: Why only 29% of students mastered LLM problem-solving—and what the other 71% couldn't figure out - an analysis of 535 students at IIT Madras Tools in Data Science, November 2025
stories.summary: Performance data from 535 students reveals a steep capability cliff in LLM problem-solving: a minority internalized the workflow, while most never learned the hidden moves needed to finish.

stories.title: Market Mix Modeling Insights
stories.description: Rather than wasting millions pushing past saturation points, invest in brand equity and pulsing spend during high-leverage moments.
stories.summary: Market mix modeling shows the real waste is not under-spending but pushing saturated channels too far, making a case for brand investment, pulsed campaigns, and timing-sensitive allocation.

stories.title: Michelin Star Restaurants
stories.description: An analysis of 22,000 Michelin star restaurants to uncover trends in cuisine, location and ratings.
stories.summary: Analysis of 22,000 Michelin-recognized restaurants surfaces strong geographic and cuisine patterns while challenging simplistic prestige narratives through comparisons of stars, prices, and city-level concentration.

stories.title: TDS Improvements
stories.description: An analysis of improvements in the IITM Tools in Data science course based on student performance data since 2024.
stories.summary: Exam data since 2024 identifies specific course-design problems, especially infrastructure-heavy questions and timing effects, and turns them into concrete teaching and assessment improvements for Tools in Data Science.

stories.title: Code Review of Shubham's GitHub
stories.description: A comprehensive review of all repositories committed to in 2025 under Shubham's GitHub account, analyzing code quality, architecture, and technical debt.
stories.summary: A cross-repository code review finds exposed credentials, missing error handling, repository sprawl, and duplicated experiments, turning one developer's 2025 GitHub output into a map of technical debt and risk.

stories.title: The Publisher Who Chose to Shrink
stories.description: How Frontiers deliberately cut output by 36% to fight AI-generated fraud and won with quality. A counterintuitive victory in academic publishing.
stories.summary: Frontiers cut output by 36% to fight paper-mill fraud and still improved quality, offering a rare publishing story where shrinking volume became the winning strategy.

stories.title: The Jobs We Refuse to Give Away
stories.description: Why some occupations resist AI not because machines can't do them, but because we believe they shouldn't. Based on Friis & Riley (2025).
stories.summary: Some jobs resist AI not because automation is impossible, but because people judge those uses morally repugnant, separating technical feasibility from social permission.

stories.title: ISS vs Tokyo
stories.description: The ISS never passes over Tokyo at midnight UTC, but touches Auckland over 30 times over 321 days. No conspiracy. Just the timing.
stories.summary: Tokyo's zero midnight-UTC ISS passes and Auckland's repeated ones look conspiratorial until orbital timing explains them, making a lesson in deterministic sampling rather than hidden anomalies.

stories.title: The Great Inversion
stories.description: Volume != Influence. Questions are liked less but engaged with more. And more insights from a Generative AI WhatsApp Group
stories.summary: In a generative-AI WhatsApp group, volume does not equal influence: quieter members attract more replies, and questions earn deeper discussion even when they receive fewer likes.

stories.title: IMDb's Hidden Algorithm Bias
stories.description: New popular movies on IMDb are punished - not by the algorithm, but the casual movie watchers rating movies lower than devotees.
stories.summary: IMDb's biggest hits are often rated lower not because the algorithm punishes popularity, but because mass audiences dilute the enthusiasm of devoted fans.

stories.title: India's Renewable Energy Revolution
stories.description: A narrative of REI Expo 2025 exhibitors: domestic players, solar dominance, and China collaboration.
stories.summary: Exhibitor data from REI Expo 2025 shows India's renewable push is domestically led and solar-heavy, yet still deeply entangled with Chinese suppliers and cross-border manufacturing links.

stories.title: GDPVal: AI Augmentation
stories.description: Explore occupations most suitable for AI augmentation based on OpenAI's GDPVal exercise
stories.summary: OpenAI's GDPVal framework maps which occupations are best suited for AI augmentation, linking task exposure to wages and showing where assistance may be more plausible than full replacement.

stories.title: Rabbit Holes
stories.description: An interactive map of 3,560 browsing chains showing how sparks turn into deep dives across the web.
stories.summary: Browsing chains from 3,560 journeys show how small sparks turn into deep dives, revealing the momentum, branching, and attention drift behind web rabbit holes.

stories.title: Do Questions Find Answers?
stories.description: Search journeys from query to first click, with filters that expose instant wins and wandering quests.
stories.summary: Search-funnel analysis tracks queries to first clicks, separating instant answers from wandering quests and showing which searches resolve quickly versus spiral into exploration.

stories.title: Your Attention Clock
stories.description: Heatmaps of weekly browsing reveal circadian focus, daily ebbs, and the domains that anchor attention.
stories.summary: Weekly attention heatmaps expose circadian browsing rhythms and the domains that anchor focus, turning raw browser logs into a clockface of recurring habits.

stories.title: The Digital Life of Anand
stories.description: An exposé of 97k visits across 84 days that charts top destinations, hourly habits, and AI-heavy searches.
stories.summary: An 84-day browser-history expose maps 97,000 visits into destinations, hourly routines, and AI-heavy behavior, revealing how one person's online life fragments across work, search, and curiosity.

stories.title: Scraping SEC
stories.description: Narrative walkthrough of how Codex CLI built an SEC scraper in one-shot, recovering from errors, handling messy data, and with self-critique.
stories.summary: A one-shot Codex session builds an SEC scraper, recovers from messy filings and errors, and documents the full workflow, making agentic coding auditable rather than magical.

stories.title: Bollywood Box Office Champions
stories.description: Interactive, inflation-adjusted bubble chart of the top-grossing Hindi films from 1994 to 2024, spotlighting record-setting blockbusters.
stories.summary: An inflation-adjusted view of Hindi box-office champions shows how record-setting films, sequels, and post-pandemic releases reshaped the league table from 1994 to 2024.

stories.title: Google Search Topic Trends
stories.description: Categorized every Google Search since Jan 2021 into 50 topics. It's mostly tech, AI, and geo-cultural. I also need to allocate more time to testing, databases, and other 'spiky' topics.
stories.summary: Four years of Google searches, clustered into 50 topics, reveal an AI gravity well, three distinct curiosity personas, and surprising search energy still devoted to Indian celebrities.

stories.title: ChatGPT vs Google
stories.description: How my ChatGPT usage has grown at the expense of Google usage. Google is only 60% of my usage, and far lower in engagement
stories.summary: Monthly usage shows ChatGPT steadily displacing Google in one person's workflow, with stronger engagement per interaction and a clear substitution pattern rather than complementary use.

stories.title: My Vipassana Experience
stories.description: A manually LLM-generated comic story book about my 10-day Vipassana meditation program, generated purely using a set of simple captions, via ChatGPT
stories.summary: A caption-driven ChatGPT comic retells a 10-day Vipassana retreat through monastic routines, silence, bodily discomfort, and pragmatic introspection, showing how simple prompts can become a coherent visual memoir.

stories.title: My Vipassana Experience
stories.description: A programmatically LLM-generated comic story book about my 10-day Vipassana meditation program, generated purely using a set of simple captions, via Gemini 2.0 Flash
stories.summary: A Gemini-generated comic recounts a 10-day Vipassana retreat as disciplined hostel life, sensory observation, and self-discovery, emphasizing the method's pragmatic, low-dogma approach to craving and aversion.

stories.title: ChatGPT Topic Trends
stories.description: Categorized the 6,000 ChatGPT conversations I've had in the last 2 years to understand what topics I discuss the most. It's mostly tech, AI, reading/writing, and some daily-life stuff.
stories.summary: Topic modeling 6,000 ChatGPT conversations reveals a code-heavy prompt portfolio, growing AI-and-business brainstorming, and telling blind spots in areas like finance, music, and philosophy.

stories.title: Indian High Courts Judgment Analysis
stories.description: Comprehensive analysis of 16M judgments from 25 Indian High Courts. Reveals court efficiency disparities, seasonal justice patterns, and systematic UAPA bail delays with 120+ day gaps between hearings.
stories.summary: Analysis of 16 million judgments across 25 Indian High Courts exposes efficiency gaps, seasonal slowdowns, and especially long UAPA bail delays that turn procedural patterns into justice outcomes.

stories.title: LLM Agents in Software: Code vs Domain
stories.description: Coding agents reduce the effort of coding. Does that mean domain will matter more? Here's an LLM-generated animation showing my perspective that domain agents will level the field.
stories.summary: An animated argument about software agents suggests coding automation will not make domain expertise singularly decisive because domain agents can lower that barrier too, expanding demand on both sides.

stories.title: Deep Research Horoscope Contradictions
stories.description: Asked Gemini Deep Research to read Sagittarius horoscope for 16 June 2025 and list contradictions from various Indian media sources.
stories.summary: Comparing Sagittarius horoscopes across Indian outlets for a single day uncovers direct contradictions in advice, tone, and predictions, exposing the fragmented authorship and media economics behind daily astrology.

stories.title: Employment Growth Since 1980
stories.description: Some US sectors like Scenic Transportation & Healthcare grew over 2X while Rail & Central Banks shrank to 40-80% of original size. Analysis using BLS CES data.
stories.summary: BLS employment data since 1980 shows some sectors doubling while others shrink dramatically, tracing a long shift toward services and away from industries more exposed to automation and structural change.

stories.title: Weight Journey 2025
stories.description: Lost 22 kg in 22 weeks through intermittent fasting. Skipped lunch, no snacks, no extra exercise.
stories.summary: A personal time series of 22 kg lost in 22 weeks links intermittent fasting to steady decline, while travel disruptions and moving averages reveal how routines shape progress.
